authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.91, Product Support Management for the Adaptive Acquisition Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This instruction sets sustainment-product support obligations and lifecycle responsibilities for defense acquisition programs, directly governing maintenance ownership, readiness planning assumptions, and source dependencies.
used_for: escalation points to engineering or mission approval authorities

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 4140.01, DoD Supply Chain Materiel Management Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This policy governs materiel visibility, supply dependencies, and logistics evidence that directly determines whether maintenance can close or be deferred.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Parts, supply, and readiness dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 3110.05, Readiness-Based Materiel Condition Reporting for Mission Essential Systems and Equipment
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This directive informs mission-capable status semantics and material condition reporting, which define readiness thresholds and operational impact of maintenance outcomes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Readiness staging, trigger interpretation, and return-to-service transitions
used_for: evidence required for status state transitions

authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This instruction provides lifecycle governance and handoff context across sustainment, safety, and engineering control domains, informing authority boundaries for return-to-service decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: DoDM 4000.25, Department of Defense Logistics Management of Materiel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This DLA policy family anchors maintenance and logistics record expectations used in repair handoff workflows, supportability status, and parts visibility.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 55000 series Asset Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized standard provides the asset-management structure for evidence continuity, lifecycle state definitions, and maintenance evidence quality expectations.

workflow_stages.name: Maintenance trigger recognition
workflow_stages.description: Classify incoming condition, fault, maintenance, and readiness signals into maintenance/lifecycle states and confirm whether sufficient trigger evidence is present.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Maintenance planning and dependency prioritization
workflow_stages.description: Assess capacity, safety constraints, parts readiness, and policy requirements before recommending sequencing and closure timelines.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Return-to-service and escalation decision
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate whether closeout, safety, and engineering conditions support return-to-service and route to adjacent authorities when prerequisites remain missing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict handling and refusal boundary
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing records, policy conflicts, and adjacent-authority boundaries without overconfident recommendations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Engineering hold, safety finding, and waiver records
Tenant queue or scheduling dependency artifacts

allowed: Assess whether incoming maintenance evidence is sufficient for a bounded in-lane maintenance status recommendation.
Identify parts, safety, and scheduling blockers for transparent outage-risk planning.
Escalate return-to-service or safety/engineering blockers to adjacent authorities when mandatory records or approvals are missing.
escalate: Parts shortages or dependency states that materially defer return-to-service
Conflicting readiness interpretation between public baseline and tenant policy
New safety/engineering findings introduced after provisional readiness planning
refuse: Final return-to-service approval without explicit approver evidence
Unresolved legal, clinical, safety-critical, or financial-authority requests
Any request to perform final engineering redesign, safety signoff, or warranty override

domain_failure_modes: Treating degraded mission-ready signals as recoverable without complete diagnostic evidence.
Returning assets to service when parts shortages or backlog blockers are unresolved.
Ignoring newly introduced safety or engineering findings in favor of planned completion.
Over-applying generic maintenance readiness language while tenant-specific rules differ by mission program.
Escalating to or acting as legal/commercial/warranty authority without explicit handoff.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Maintenance delayed by missing parts, approvals, or safety checks
Asset requested back into service without required signoff
Safety or engineering judgment changes the next action

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific interpretation of mission-capable state terms
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: National-level readiness semantics differ from each tenant's mission doctrine and authority map.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve tenant state dictionaries and readiness thresholds before classifying final in-lane recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Authority threshold for legal or financial actions in adjacent specialists
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public sources do not replace tenant-specific policy for legal/commercial/governance actions.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Route those requests through adjacent-lane mapping when threshold is unclear or exceeded.

notes: Research contract is applied through named defense maintenance and readiness sources plus ISO asset-management structure for evidence posture.
Source mix covers workflow, dependencies, boundaries, and failure handling as required by the contract and queue-level requirements.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; all gaps are flagged as non-blocking and required as tenant retrieval prior to confident recommendations.
